blocks.content: After lunch they spread out in the backyard. Eli was on his back in the grass identifying clouds. Savannah had a book somewhere on her — she was always prepared.

Kit sat on the back steps with his knees up. Daisy sat on the top step beside him. Millhaven sounded like this in the afternoon: a lawn mower three streets over, the box fan, a radio too faint to make out the song.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You were looking at your mom's street up there.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: She's fine. Working Tuesdays and Thursdays now. The diner.
He picked at a spot on his knee.

blocks.speaker: Kit
blocks.content: I think she comes home tired.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: My dad used to work doubles at the hardware store. Before he got the supervisor thing. He'd come home and just — not be able to talk for about an hour. Mama called it refilling.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Himself, I guess. All the parts that talking to people all day uses up.
Kit thought about his mom at the kitchen table. Glass of water, fifteen minutes. Nobody talking.
They were quiet for a minute. Eli had found a cloud shaped like a boat and was explaining this to the sky in some detail.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: It's good that she has something. The diner. Even if it's tiring.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: You want to go to Callaway Park later?
